title: why is apple watch second and fenix thirdrd

messages.content: why is apple watch second and fenix thirdrd

I only have detailed information for the Apple Watch Series 11 in my knowledge base — I don’t have any data for a Garmin Fenix model, so I can’t say why that Fenix was placed third. Based on the Series 11 info, here are likely reasons it lands high (e.g., second) in rankings, framed to your priorities:

- Long battery: larger cells (+9% / +11%) give about 24 hours typical use and up to 38 hours in Low Power Mode — the review says it often delivers about a day-and-a-half in real use.  
- With display: an always-on Retina LTPO3 wide-angle OLED (up to 2,000 nits) gives a bright, high-contrast screen that stays usable while saving power.  
- GPS accuracy: it supports L1 GPS plus GNSS, Galileo and BeiDou, which covers common satellite systems used for accurate tracking.  
- Sleep and health: new Sleep Score (including retroactive scoring), blood-oxygen monitoring (returned in the US), and Hypertension Notifications add solid sleep/health insight and stress-related signals.  
- Overall balance: the Series 11 improves endurance and keeps strong app and sensor performance, so it’s a good middle ground for lifestyle, running/cycling, and health features.

If you want a precise explanation of why a specific Fenix model was ranked third (or a direct head-to-head), tell me which Fenix model it is and I’ll compare them focusing on battery, GPS, stress/sleep monitoring, and the display.
